delete_modal_li_container: Container only — data volumes on the server remain; directories can be reused on the next deploy.
delete_modal_li_data: Container and data — also removes mounted directories under DEPLOY_BASE_PATH for this container (irreversible).
confirm_delete_container_only: Delete container «{name}»? Data on the server (volumes) will remain.
confirm_delete_container_with_data: Delete container «{name}» with data on disk? This cannot be undone.
empty_templates_tail: or run npm run restore-templates in the deployer directory.
empty_containers: No containers. Start a container from a template above.
err_no_template: No template selected. Close the dialog and click Deploy on the desired template.
editor_pull_ifnotpresent: ifNotPresent — pull only if not present locally
hint_import: Upload a .json template file — fields will be filled automatically. Check ID, image and parameters, then click Save template.
hint_basic:
ID — Latin letters, digits, _ and -; cannot be changed after save.
Image — full name from registry or Docker Hub.
Platform — image architecture (linux/amd64, linux/arm64).
Container name is set at deploy (UI / API), not in the template; in volumes use {{CONTAINER_NAME}}.
User — UID or uid:gid.
Pull / Restart — same as docker run. Max retries — for on-failure.
Wait for healthy — deploy succeeds only after healthcheck (requires Healthcheck in parameters).
hint_fields: Parameters for {{KEY}} substitution at deploy. Key — uppercase A–Z and _ only. If Default is empty — value must be passed in the form or API params, otherwise Missing param. In Default — literal or another substitution, including {{GEN_UUID}}.
hint_provision:
Provision — commands inside the Deployer container before docker create. The last stdout line must be JSON; keys merge into params and are available as {{KEY}}. Tools: DEPLOYER_SOFTWARE (setup-server-stack). expect — required keys from that JSON.

Multiple cards → step array; one card → object.
hint_deprovision: Deprovision — runs when deleting a container with removeData=true and templateId. Step format is the same as provision. Deprovision failure does not block removal — the response includes a warning.
hint_limits: Resource limits (HostConfig). Memory — 512m, 1g. Memory swap — swap limit (bytes or suffix). CPUs — 0.5, 2. PIDs — process limit. Empty — no limit.
hint_networks: Connect to Docker networks. Name — as in docker network ls. Aliases — DNS names in network, comma-separated. IPv4 — static address in network (if driver supports). {{KEY}} substitutions allowed.
hint_ports: Mapping: container port → host port, TCP or UDP. Same as docker run -p HOST:CONTAINER. Host port — number 1–65535 or {{HOST_PORT}}. Empty host — expose only, no publish.
hint_env: NAME=VALUE pairs inside the container. Values may include {{KEY}} and server context {{DEPLOY_BASE_PATH}}.
hint_volumes: Bind — host directory under {{DEPLOY_BASE_PATH}}. Volume — Docker named volume. Mode — rw or ro. Container path — absolute.
hint_labels: Docker labels: one line — one label as key=value. For Traefik: traefik.http.routers.xxx.rule=Host(`{{DOMAIN}}`). Values may include {{KEY}} substitutions.
hint_entrypoint: Container entrypoint (Entrypoint in Docker API). Each line — one exec array argument. Empty list — value from image. {{KEY}} substitutions allowed.
hint_command: Command arguments (Cmd in Docker API) — passed to entrypoint. Each line — one argument. Empty list — value from image. {{KEY}} substitutions allowed.
hint_docker_params:
Everything not covered above — healthcheck, security, GPU, shm, dns, etc. Two columns: parameter name (camelCase from Docker API) and value (string, number, true/false or JSON).

Scope (container or host) is determined automatically: Healthcheck, WorkingDir → inside container; SecurityOpt, ShmSize, DeviceRequests → host settings. For rare names use config. or host. prefix (config.WorkingDir).

Repeating keys — separate rows (SecurityOpt, Dns). {{KEY}} in values.

Ignored for security: Privileged, CapAdd, Devices, DeviceCgroupRules, isolation-disabling SecurityOpt values (e.g. seccomp:unconfined) and the host value for IpcMode/PidMode/UTSMode.
subst_key: {{KEY}} — value from form field or API params at deploy. E.g. {{NAME}}, {{HOST_PORT}}, {{DOMAIN}}. If substitution has no value — deploy fails with Missing param.
subst_gen_uuid: {{GEN_UUID}} — UUID v4, one value per deploy. Useful for unique container name: app-{{GEN_UUID}}.
subst_deploy_base: {{DEPLOY_BASE_PATH}} — data directory on deployer server. Set by server config, not a form field. Use in volume paths: {{DEPLOY_BASE_PATH}}/{{NAME}}/data.
subst_note: Substitutions can be nested in field defaults and combined with plain text.
err_field_key_row: Form fields, row {row}: key must be uppercase A–Z and underscore.
err_port_host_row: Ports, row {row}: host port must be {min}–{max} or {{KEY}}
err_import_not_object: JSON must be a template object
err_import_id: id required: Latin letters, digits, _ and -
err_template_id_format: ID: Latin letters, digits, _ and - only
err_rate_limit: Too many requests. Retry in {sec} s.
err_operation_not_found: Operation not found (e.g. after Deployer restart)
err_container_limit: Container limit reached: {count} / {limit}. Remove containers or increase CONTAINER_LIMIT.
mcp_lead: Key grants full Deployer API access via MCP (no roles). No password in Cursor — Bearer key only. Operator can deny specific tools via DEPLOYER_MCP_TOOLS_DENY in env.
mcp_config_hint: After issuing, set the URL below and the key in your AI tool MCP settings (e.g. Cursor).
mcp_limit_reached: Key limit reached ({limit}). Revoke an unused key.
mcp_modal_once: Copy the key now — it will not be shown again.
mcp_create_no_plaintext: Key created but value could not be shown. Refresh the page.
mcp_err_server_stale: Deployer server is outdated — restart the container (docker compose restart deployer-1) and refresh (F5).
